title: sideritis × angustifolia var. saetabensis
intro: Sideritis × angustifolia var. saetabensis, also known as Cretan mountain tea or mountain tea, is a hybrid plant species in the genus Sideritis, native to the island of Crete in Greece. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, and is known for its potential health benefits such as reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and boosting the immune system. The health benefits of Sideritis × angustifolia var. saetabensis are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can be found in various herbal preparations, including tea infusions, tinctures, and capsules. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as a tea, or extracted with a solvent to create a tincture or capsule.
